seoTitle: How to Build an Actual vs Budget Dashboard in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to build an Actual vs Budget dashboard in Power BI with variance analysis, helping finance teams track performance against targets.
intro: Comparing actual results to budgeted figures is one of the most common reporting needs in finance, but building it cleanly takes some setup. This guide walks through creating an Actual vs Budget dashboard in Power BI, including how to visualize the variance between the two. It's aimed at finance teams and managers who need a clear, reliable view of financial performance against plan.

steps.heading: Set up your data
steps.body: Bring in both your actual results and budgeted figures, structured so they can be compared side by side, typically by period and category.

steps.heading: Create the visualization
steps.body: Build charts that place actual and budget values next to each other, such as a clustered column chart or a line chart comparing the two over time.

steps.heading: Analyze the variance
steps.body: Add a variance measure that calculates the difference between actual and budget, and consider showing it as both an absolute number and a percentage.

steps.heading: Finalize the dashboard
steps.body: Bring the visuals together into a cohesive layout, adding filters for time period or department so users can explore the variance at different levels.

steps.heading: Apply it to financial reviews
steps.body: Use the finished dashboard during budget reviews or strategy meetings to quickly identify where actual performance is ahead of or behind plan.

faq.question: What's the best way to visualize variance between actual and budget?
faq.answer: A combination of a comparison chart alongside a dedicated variance column or card often works well, since it shows both the raw numbers and the gap clearly.

faq.question: Should variance be shown as a percentage or a raw number?
faq.answer: Showing both is common practice, since a raw number gives scale while a percentage makes it easier to compare variance across categories of different sizes.

faq.question: Can this dashboard update automatically as new actuals come in?
faq.answer: Yes, if your actual and budget data sources are connected with a refresh schedule, the dashboard and variance calculations will update automatically.

faq.question: How do I handle months where budget data hasn't been entered yet?
faq.answer: It helps to build in handling for blank budget values so the variance calculation doesn't show misleading results for periods without a budget set.
